en.footer.intro: NOVACORE builds robotically-calibrated power cells engineered for the workshop, the warehouse, and everything in between.
en.footer.bookNote: An engineer replies within one working day - not a brochure.
en.seo.home.title: NOVACORE - Precision Industrial Power Cells
en.seo.home.desc: Robotically calibrated cells engineered for the workshop, the warehouse, and everything in between.
en.seo.book.title: Contact Engineering - NOVACORE
en.hero.lsub: Robotically calibrated cells engineered for the workshop, the warehouse, and everything in between.
en.hero.rsub: Every cell is hand-selected, robot-tested and serialized - so your tools never miss a beat on site.
en.prod.title: Four cells.
en.prod.intro: Each NOVACORE cell is built to survive a worst-day-on-site test before it ever earns a serial number. Pick the size - we already handled the punishment.
en.tech.title: The four systems that
en.tech.intro: Tools fail when their power source flinches. We built four independent systems whose only job is to make sure your pack never flinches - not on day one, and not on day three thousand.
en.tech.s1.d: Every individual lithium cell is graded across 14 voltage curves, then matched into packs by an autonomous robotic line - so the weakest cell in any pack still outperforms the industry average.
en.tech.s2.d: NovaSync BMS samples each cell 400 times per second, balancing current to keep the entire pack inside a perfect thermal corridor - even on a 60°C asphalt roof.
en.tech.s3.t: Heat goes out. Cold stays out.
en.tech.s3.d: A phase-change polymer skin absorbs heat spikes during peak draw and re-radiates it during idle. Same cells, 30% longer service life on heavy-duty tools.
en.tech.s4.t: Built like the tools it powers.
en.tech.s4.d: A glass-filled nylon mono-shell tested at 2.4m drops onto raw concrete, then back into service - no recalibration, no re-pairing. Just keep working.
en.res.title: We document everything
en.res.intro: Engineering write-ups, field journals, and the occasional bit of press. No fluff - just what the team learned this week.
en.res.a1.t: What 14 months on a North-Sea rig taught us about IP-ratings.
en.res.a1.d: Sixty packs went out. Sixty came back. Here's everything our crew measured between sunrise pours and 4am gale tests.
en.res.a2.t: Why we sample every cell 400 times a second (and why slower hurts).
en.res.a2.d: A look inside the firmware loop that lets NovaSync BMS detect a failing cell three weeks before its voltage ever wobbles.
en.res.a3.t: NOVACORE selected as battery partner for the 2027 Solar Challenge.
en.res.a3.d: Twenty student teams will run our Forge 36V packs in 48°C desert heat - and yes, we're sending engineers along for the ride.
en.res.a4.t: Concrete drop tests, on purpose - the case for redundant shells.
en.res.a4.d: We dropped 240 packs onto raw concrete from 2.4 metres. Here's what survived, what didn't, and what we changed.
en.res.a5.t: Three winters in Yukon: lithium chemistry meets -42°C reality.
en.res.a5.d: A heavy-equipment fleet ran our Titan XL through three full Canadian winters. The data turned out almost too clean to believe.
en.res.a6.t: Forge 36V wins iF Industrial Design Award 2026.
en.res.a6.d: Recognized for engineering integrity and field-first ergonomics. The jury cited 'unmistakable purpose in every surface'.
